items.title: Attract more donors
items.text: Transparency reassures. Donors prefer organizations that show how their funds are used.

items.title: Simplify your reporting
items.text: Receipts and expense proofs are recorded automatically. Less paperwork, more fieldwork.

items.title: Zero technical skills needed
items.text: You manage your association in euros, as usual. The technology is invisible — we handle everything.

form.success: Thank you! Your association is on the waitlist. We'll contact you as soon as the platform launches.

hero.description: Join the first pilot associations of Common Link. We support you in simplifying your management and strengthening the trust of your supporters.
hero.title: Offer transparency to your donors

search.description: Start by searching for your association in the official directory. This allows us to verify your organization exists and pre-fill your information.

search.title: Register your association

cta.description: Join the first donors who want to know exactly where their money goes.
cta.title: Ready to give differently?

items.question: Are my donations tax-deductible?
items.answer: Yes. For each donation to an eligible association, you receive a valid tax receipt allowing you to benefit from the tax reduction provided by law (66% of the amount, up to 20% of taxable income for individuals).

items.question: How can I track my donation?
items.answer: From your dashboard, you can see in real time the expenses related to the projects you support. Each expense comes with verifiable proof: receipts, dates, amounts.

items.question: When do I receive my tax receipt?
items.answer: Instantly. As soon as your donation is confirmed, the tax receipt is available in your account. No more waiting for the association's mail at the end of the year.

items.question: Can I donate to multiple associations?
items.answer: Of course. You can support as many partner associations as you wish. Each donation is tracked individually and you receive a tax receipt per association.

items.question: Is my data protected?
items.answer: Absolutely. We comply with GDPR and never share your personal data with third parties. Your tax information is encrypted and used solely to generate your receipts.

hero.description: Track the use of every euro donated. Monitor in real time the expenses of the associations you support, with verifiable proof.
hero.title: Give with confidence

items.title: Real-time tracking
items.text: See where your money goes, step by step. Every expense is linked to a specific project.

items.title: Digital tax receipt
items.text: Receive your tax receipt instantly in your account. No more waiting months.

items.title: Verified history
items.text: View all your donations and their impact. Secure proof, accessible at any time.

signup.note: Your data is collected solely to inform you of the launch. It will not be shared or used for other purposes. You can exercise your rights (access, rectification, deletion) by contacting us at contact@liencommun.fr.
signup.successText: We'll let you know as soon as the platform is available. Keep an eye on your inbox.

brand.description: Transparency on donation usage, made possible by blockchain technology.
brand.name: Common Link

footer.luciole: Typeface <link>Luciole</link> by the Centre Technique Régional pour la Déficience Visuelle and typographies.fr — CC BY 4.0 license.

associations.text: "Proving our good management takes forever." Associations spend hundreds of hours producing reports to reassure their funders.
associations.title: Association side

donors.text: "I don't know how my money is being used." 6 out of 10 donors want more transparency on how their donations are used.
donors.title: Donor side

items.question: Do I need to understand blockchain to use Common Link?
items.answer: No, absolutely not. Common Link is designed to be as simple as a bank transfer. The blockchain works in the background, you never need to interact with it directly.

items.question: How are donations tracked?
items.answer: Each donation is converted to stablecoin (EUR CoinVertible) and recorded on the Ethereum blockchain. Each association expense is also recorded with a timestamped receipt.

items.question: Do I receive a tax receipt?
items.answer: Yes. Each donation automatically generates a digital tax receipt in the form of an NFT, which you can download or keep in your donor space.

items.question: Which associations can register?
items.answer: Any association registered in the SIRENE directory can register for free. Verification is automatic.

items.question: How much does it cost?
items.answer: Registration is free for associations and donors. Common Link charges a 2.5% commission on each donation to fund the platform and blockchain fees.

items.question: Is the data secure?
items.answer: Yes. Personal data is stored encrypted and GDPR compliant. Only transaction proofs are public on the blockchain (without personal data).

items.title: Total transparency
items.text: Every donation is recorded with unfalsifiable cryptographic proof. Donors see exactly where their money goes.

items.title: Verifiable proof
items.text: Every expense comes with a timestamped and verified receipt. No more opaque reports.

items.title: Total simplicity
items.text: No need to understand blockchain. Donors give in euros, associations spend in euros.

guarantee.text: Our technology relies on the Ethereum blockchain and regulated stablecoins. Every transaction is immutable, publicly verifiable, and compliant with European regulations.
guarantee.title: Traceability guarantee

hero.subtitle: We make the link between impact and generosity visible
hero.title: Transparency on donation usage

cgu.intro: These terms and conditions of use define the conditions for accessing and using the site https://www.common-link.org/ and the services offered by CommonLink.

s1.campaign: Campaign: page or dedicated space for a fundraising operation presented by a beneficiary organisation on the Platform.
s1.commonlink: CommonLink: the company CommonLink, SAS, registered with the RCS of Antibes under number 105 153 928, whose registered office is at 1047 Chemin des Impiniers, 06220 Vallauris, France.
s1.donor: Donor: any person making a donation for the benefit of a beneficiary organisation via the Platform.
s1.intro: In these terms and conditions, the terms below have the following meaning:
s1.org: Beneficiary organisation: any association, foundation, endowment fund, NGO or eligible structure using CommonLink to present a campaign, receive donations or document the use of collected funds.
s1.platform: Platform: the digital environment offered by CommonLink enabling beneficiary organisations to present fundraising campaigns and donors to make, track and document their donations.
s1.site: Site: the website accessible at https://www.common-link.org/.
s1.title: 1. Definitions
s1.user: User: any person accessing the Site or using CommonLink services.

s10.p1: When making a donation, the donor may leave a voluntary contribution to CommonLink. This contribution is optional and can be reduced to zero before confirming payment.
s10.title: 10. Voluntary contribution to CommonLink

s11.p1: CommonLink charges beneficiary organisations service fees of 8% excl. VAT and payment and technical fees of 2% of donations collected, invoiced monthly.
s11.p2: Applicable fees are detailed on the Pricing page of the Site.
s11.title: 11. Fees applicable to beneficiary organisations

s12.item5: beneficiary organisation removed from the Platform;
s12.p1: Donations are in principle final and non-refundable. A refund may however be considered in the following cases:
s12.p2: Requests should be sent to: contact@common-link.org
s12.title: 12. Refunds

s13.p1: When the beneficiary organisation is eligible, CommonLink generates and transmits tax receipts on behalf of the beneficiary organisation.
s13.p2: Digital certificates, NFTs or complementary proof elements do not constitute tax receipts.
s13.title: 13. Tax receipts

s14.p1: CommonLink provides transparency tools to track certain financial flows and present proof of use based on recorded transactions.
s14.p2: CommonLink does not guarantee that every euro collected is fully traceable in all situations, and does not guarantee the final use of funds by the beneficiary organisation.
s14.title: 14. Transparency and proof of use

s15.p1: Beneficiary organisations wishing to use CommonLink must create an account and provide the information and documents necessary for their identification and validation.
s15.p2: CommonLink may validate, refuse, suspend or remove a beneficiary organisation or campaign in particular in cases of inaccurate information, KYC/KYB refusal, suspicion of fraud or legal risk.
s15.title: 15. Terms applicable to beneficiary organisations

s16.p1: The beneficiary organisation grants CommonLink a non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide licence to host, reproduce and distribute content transmitted for the purposes of the service.
s16.title: 16. Content published by beneficiary organisations

s17.p1: The contract between CommonLink and the beneficiary organisation is concluded for an indefinite period. Either party may terminate with 30 days' notice, subject to the processing of ongoing transactions.
s17.p2: CommonLink may immediately suspend a beneficiary organisation's access in cases of fraud, false information or legal risk.
s17.title: 17. Termination and suspension of beneficiary organisations

s18.p1: The user undertakes to use the Site and Platform in accordance with the law and these terms. It is in particular prohibited to use the Site for fraudulent purposes, to impersonate an identity, to disrupt the Site's operation or to circumvent security measures.
s18.title: 18. User obligations

s19.p1: The Site, the Platform, the CommonLink brand and all content published by CommonLink are protected by applicable intellectual property law. Any unauthorised reproduction or exploitation is prohibited.
s19.title: 19. Intellectual property

s2.item0: presentation of beneficiary organisations and campaigns;
s2.item4: generation and transmission of tax receipts on behalf of beneficiary organisations, when applicable conditions are met;
s2.item6: access to transparency and proof-of-use elements.
s2.p1: CommonLink is a donation platform enabling associations, foundations, endowment funds, NGOs and corporate donors to collect, track and document the use of collected funds, with transparency and proof-of-use mechanisms.
s2.title: 2. Service description

s20.p1: Information on personal data processing is described in the Privacy Policy accessible on the Site.
s20.title: 20. Personal data

s21.p1: Applicable rules are described in the Cookie Policy accessible on the Site.
s21.title: 21. Cookies

s22.p1: CommonLink implements reasonable measures to ensure the Site functions correctly but does not guarantee continuous or error-free availability.
s22.title: 22. Availability and security

s23.p1: CommonLink does not guarantee the success of a campaign or the final use of funds by the beneficiary organisation. CommonLink shall not be liable for indirect damages or malfunctions attributable to third-party providers.
s23.title: 23. Limitation of liability

s24.p1: CommonLink shall not be liable for non-performance resulting from force majeure or an event reasonably beyond its control.
s24.title: 24. Force majeure

s25.response: CommonLink aims to respond within 48 business hours.
s25.title: 25. Contact

s26.p1: These terms and conditions are governed by French law. For disputes between CommonLink and a professional beneficiary organisation, jurisdiction is attributed to the commercial court of Antibes, unless a mandatory rule provides otherwise.
s26.title: 26. Applicable law

s3.p1: CommonLink is a social and solidarity economy company. An ESUS accreditation application has been submitted.
s3.p2: CommonLink is in the process of registering with ORIAS as an intermediary in participatory financing.
s3.p3: CommonLink is not a payment institution. Payment services are provided by authorised providers.
s3.p4: Donations are made directly for the benefit of beneficiary organisations. CommonLink does not legally collect the funds for its own account.
s3.title: 3. CommonLink's status

s4.p1: Accessing the Site implies acceptance of these terms and conditions of use.
s4.p2: To make a donation or create an account, the user must expressly accept these terms and conditions.
s4.p3: CommonLink may modify these terms and conditions to reflect changes to the service, regulations, technical partners or provider terms of use. The applicable version is the one in force at the time of use.
s4.title: 4. Acceptance of the terms and conditions

s5.p1: The Site is freely accessible to any user with an internet connection. Certain features require account creation.
s5.p2: CommonLink may suspend, limit or interrupt access to the Site or Platform for maintenance, security, updates, force majeure or any other legitimate reason.
s5.title: 5. Access to the Site and Platform

s6.p1: Creating a donor account is required to make a donation via the Platform.
s6.p2: The donor undertakes to provide accurate, complete and up-to-date information and is responsible for the confidentiality of their credentials.
s6.p3: In the event of unauthorised use of their account, the donor must notify CommonLink without delay at: contact@common-link.org
s6.title: 6. Creating a donor account

s7.p1: The Platform is intended for adults. Minors may only use the Platform with the authorisation of their legal representative.
s7.p2: The donor chooses a campaign presented on the Platform and makes a donation for the benefit of the relevant beneficiary organisation. Selecting a campaign results in a global allocation of the donation to that campaign.
s7.title: 7. Terms applicable to donors

s8.p1: Donations made via CommonLink are one-off at launch. Recurring donation features may be offered later.
s8.p2: Donations are made in euros. The donation is legally vested in the beneficiary organisation upon receipt, regardless of whether the displayed campaign goal is reached.
s8.title: 8. Donations

s9.p1: Payment methods offered at launch are bank card and SEPA direct debit. Payments are processed by one or more authorised payment service providers.
s9.p2: CommonLink does not collect, store or directly process full credit card data.
s9.title: 9. Payment

faq.intro: Find below answers to the most frequently asked questions about CommonLink.

q0.a: CommonLink is a donation platform enabling associations, foundations, endowment funds, NGOs and corporate donors to collect, track and document the use of collected funds, with transparency and proof-of-use mechanisms.

q1.a: CommonLink is aimed at associations, foundations, endowment funds, NGOs, corporate donors, and donors wishing to track how their funds are used. The service targets the French market at launch.

q10.a: In principle, a donation is final and non-refundable. A refund may however be considered in cases of double payment, manifest error, fraud, unauthorised payment, cancelled campaign or beneficiary organisation removed from the Platform. Requests should be sent to contact@common-link.org.

q11.a: Unless stated otherwise, the donation is legally vested in the beneficiary organisation upon receipt, regardless of whether the displayed goal is reached.
q11.q: What happens if a campaign does not reach its goal?

q12.a: The donation is made directly for the benefit of the beneficiary organisation. CommonLink does not legally collect the funds for its own account.

q13.a: CommonLink charges beneficiary organisations 8% excl. VAT in service fees and 2% in payment and technical processing fees, invoiced monthly. The donor may also leave an optional voluntary contribution. See the Pricing page for details.
q13.q: Does CommonLink take a commission on donations?

q14.a: When the beneficiary organisation is eligible, tax receipts are generated by CommonLink on behalf of the beneficiary organisation. They may be sent by email and/or made available in the donor's account.

q15.a: No. A digital certificate, NFT or complementary proof element does not constitute a tax receipt. A tax receipt is a separate document, generated on behalf of the beneficiary organisation when applicable conditions are met.
q15.q: Is the digital certificate or NFT a tax receipt?

q16.a: No. CommonLink does not guarantee the final use of funds by the beneficiary organisation. CommonLink provides transparency tools to track certain financial flows and present proof of use.
q16.q: Does CommonLink guarantee how funds are used?

q17.a: Proof of use refers to elements that document how funds were used: recorded transactions, submitted supporting documents, technical or blockchain data when these features are activated. These elements do not constitute exhaustive accounting or legal certification.

q18.a: Certain CommonLink features may use blockchain technologies, including Polygon, to contribute to the transparency and documentation of flows.

q19.a: Yes. CommonLink may use Monerium's services for certain features related to electronic money, EURe and flow transparency. Monerium is an authorised electronic money institution.

q2.a: Yes. CommonLink is a social and solidarity economy company. An ESUS accreditation application has been submitted.
q2.q: Is CommonLink a social and solidarity economy company?

q20.a: Yes. CommonLink may refuse, suspend or remove a beneficiary organisation or campaign in particular in cases of inaccurate information, KYC/KYB refusal, suspicion of fraud, serious dispute or legal risk.
q20.q: Can CommonLink refuse or suspend an organisation?

q21.a: At launch, CommonLink uses only cookies strictly necessary for the Site to function. Analytics, marketing or advertising cookies are not active at launch. See the Cookie Policy.

q22.a: Email: contact@common-link.org — Phone: 06 31 63 60 08. CommonLink aims to respond within 48 business hours.

q23.a: To exercise your rights regarding your personal data, write to legal@common-link.org. CommonLink aims to respond within 7 business days.
q23.q: How do I exercise my GDPR rights?

q3.a: CommonLink is in the process of registering with ORIAS as an intermediary in participatory financing.

q4.a: No. CommonLink is not a payment institution. Payment services are provided by authorised providers.

q5.a: The donor creates an account, selects a campaign, makes a donation, receives a payment confirmation and can track their donation from their account. When applicable conditions are met, a tax receipt is generated by CommonLink on behalf of the beneficiary organisation.

q6.a: No. Creating an account is required to make a donation via CommonLink.
q6.q: Can I donate without creating an account?

q7.a: No. The donor chooses a campaign but does not choose a detailed individual allocation of funds. The allocation is global to the selected campaign.
q7.q: Can I choose exactly how my donation is used?

q8.a: Donations are one-off at launch. Recurring donation features may be offered later.

q9.a: At launch, CommonLink plans to accept bank cards and SEPA direct debit. Other payment methods may be added later.

faq.title: FAQ

politiqueConfidentialite.intro: CommonLink places great importance on the protection of personal data. This Privacy Policy explains what personal data is collected, for what purposes, on what legal bases, for how long it is retained, with whom it may be shared and what rights can be exercised.

legalBasis.intro: Processing is based, as applicable, on:
legalBasis.item0: performance of a contract or pre-contractual measures;
legalBasis.item2: CommonLink's legitimate interests (security, fraud prevention, B2B prospecting);
legalBasis.item3: the data subject's consent (newsletter, certain cookies);
legalBasis.item4: the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims.
legalBasis.title: 4. Legal bases

newsletter.p1: CommonLink may send a newsletter to those who have given their consent (opt-in). Each email contains an unsubscribe link.
newsletter.p2: CommonLink may carry out B2B prospecting targeting organisations likely to be interested in its services.
newsletter.title: 8. Newsletter and prospecting

purposes.intro: Personal data may be processed for:
purposes.item0: creating and managing user accounts and beneficiary organisations;
purposes.item10: sending newsletters to those who have given their consent.
purposes.item3: providing transparency information and proof of use;
purposes.item4: KYC, KYB compliance, anti-fraud and applicable obligations;
purposes.item7: compliance with legal and regulatory obligations;
purposes.item9: B2B prospecting for organisations likely to use CommonLink;
purposes.title: 3. Processing purposes

recipients.intro: Personal data may be shared, as applicable, with:
recipients.item1: beneficiary organisations concerned by the donations;
recipients.item5: technical, transactional email and support providers;
recipients.item6: administrative, judicial or tax authorities where required by law.
recipients.monerium: CommonLink may use Monerium's services for certain features related to electronic money and flow transparency.
recipients.title: 5. Data recipients

retention.row0col2: Duration of account existence, then archiving if necessary
retention.row1col1: Donation data, tax receipts and accounting obligations
retention.row1col2: Up to 10 years from the close of the relevant financial year
retention.row2col2: Duration of the contractual relationship, then archiving per legal obligations
retention.row3col2: 6 to 12 months, unless longer retention is necessary in the event of an incident
retention.row4col2: 3 years from the last active contact
retention.row5col2: Until consent is withdrawn or 3 years after the last contact
retention.row6col2: Time needed for processing, then evidentiary archiving up to 5 years
retention.title: 7. Retention periods

rights.contact: These rights may be exercised by writing to: legal@common-link.org
rights.intro: In accordance with applicable regulations, any data subject may exercise:
rights.item6: right to withdraw consent at any time;
rights.item7: right to define directives regarding the fate of data after death.
rights.response: CommonLink aims to respond within 7 business days.
rights.title: 11. Data subject rights

security.p1: CommonLink implements reasonable technical and organisational measures to protect personal data: access control, secure hosting, encryption, logging, role separation, restriction of access to authorised persons.
security.title: 10. Security

politiqueConfidentialite.title: Privacy Policy

transfers.p1: CommonLink favours providers located in the European Union. At launch, CommonLink does not intend to transfer personal data outside the European Union.

consent.title: 5. Consent

duration.p1: Strictly necessary cookies are retained for a period limited to what is necessary for their purpose. Exact durations may vary depending on the type of cookie used.

whatIs.p1: A cookie is a small file placed or read on the user's device when they visit a website. Cookies can serve different purposes: enabling a site to function correctly, securing a session, remembering preferences, measuring audience or personalising content.
whatIs.title: 1. What is a cookie?

billing.p1: Fees owed by beneficiary organisations are invoiced monthly, after donations are collected. Invoices are issued by CommonLink and transmitted to the beneficiary organisation according to agreed terms.

donors.p1: Using CommonLink is free for donors.
donors.p2: When making a donation, the donor may leave a voluntary contribution to CommonLink. This contribution is optional. It may be dynamically suggested or pre-filled, but the donor can modify it or reduce it to zero before confirming the payment. The voluntary contribution is expressed including all taxes.

architecture.description: Every donation is tracked end-to-end. Blockchain technology serves as a proof ledger, but remains invisible to end users.

steps.title: 1. Receiving the donation in euros
steps.text: The donor gives in euros through the platform. Zero friction, zero crypto jargon.

steps.title: 2. Conversion to EURCV
steps.text: Funds are converted to euro stablecoin (EURCV) via our institutional partner, ensuring compliance and auditability.

steps.title: 3. On-chain transfer to the association
steps.text: EURCV tokens are transferred to the association's wallet. Every movement is recorded on the blockchain.

steps.title: 4. Allocation & expenses
steps.text: The association distributes funds among its members. Every payment is tracked, with EURCV → EUR reconversion for bank transfers.

steps.title: 5. Proofs & NFT tax receipt
steps.text: At each step, a proof is recorded on-chain. The donor receives their tax receipt as an NFT, accessible at any time.

architecture.title: Architecture & fund flow
